name: The Heron's Clam Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 cup light cream or half-and-half, or as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the salt pork in a large Dutch oven until crisp. Use a slotted spoon to remove and set aside.
2. Add onions and celery to the drippings, mixing well.
3. Add potatoes and 2 cups water (or enough to almost cover the vegetables). Cover and simmer for 18-20 minutes, or until the potatoes and other vegetables are soft.
4. Stir in the clams and bring to a boil.
5. Blend flour with 2 cups water until smooth.
6. Add the flour mixture to the soup and cook, stirring constantly, until smooth and thickened, about 2 minutes.
7. Stir in evaporated milk, butter, salt, white pepper, thyme, rosemary, hot pepper sauce, and reserved pork bits.
8. Heat gently; do not boil. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
9. Serve in warmed soup bowls and add light cream or half-and-half to thin as desired.
